items.text: Notice: Water fountains on the third floor of Anderson Hall will be unavailable on March 12 from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM for plumbing maintenance. A temporary water station will be set up near Room 304. A $5 deposit is required for reusable bottles from Facilities Office staff.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to borrow a reusable bottle?

options.A: A signed maintenance form from Facilities

questions.explanation: The notice states a $5 deposit is required for reusable bottles from Facilities Office staff.

questions.stem: Where will the temporary water station be located?

options.A: Outside Anderson Hall by the entrance
options.B: On the fourth floor of Anderson Hall
options.C: Near Room 304 on the third floor

questions.explanation: The notice specifies the temporary water station will be set up near Room 304.

items.text: Notice: Water fountains on the second floor of Holloway Hall will be turned off from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM on April 4 for filter replacement. Building occupants should use the fountains on the first or third floor during this six-hour window. Maintenance staff will post signs at each affected fountain.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should occupants do during the closure?

options.A: Submit a maintenance form before arriving
options.B: Bring sealed water bottles to the second floor
options.C: Report to Facilities Office for replacements
options.D: Use fountains on the first or third floor

questions.explanation: The notice instructs occupants to use the first or third floor fountains during the closure.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the closure?

options.A: Other floors are also being maintained the same day
options.B: Drinking water remains available somewhere in the building
options.C: The second floor will be inaccessible to all visitors
options.D: Filter replacement will continue into the next morning

questions.explanation: Since the first and third floors remain open, drinking water remains available in the building during the closure.

items.text: Dear Mr. Park, I'm confirming my campus tour guide volunteer shift on May 18 from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM. I'll meet new students at the Visitor Center desk in Bradley Hall. Please let me know if I should bring anything else. Thanks, Ana

questions.stem: According to the email, where will Ana meet the new students?

options.A: At the Visitor Center desk in Bradley Hall
options.B: At the Admissions Office near Bradley Hall
options.C: At the main library entrance on campus
options.D: At the registrar table inside the auditorium

questions.explanation: The email confirms Ana will meet new students at the Visitor Center desk in Bradley Hall.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email?

options.A: Ana has never volunteered with the campus tour program
options.B: Mr. Park typically provides volunteers with extra supplies
options.C: Ana is uncertain whether additional items are required
options.D: The Visitor Center desk closes shortly after 4:00 PM

questions.explanation: Ana asks Mr. Park to let her know if she should bring anything else, implying uncertainty about what items are required.

items.text: Hi Sara, your interlibrary loan book "Coastal Wetlands" arrived at the front desk this morning. Please pick it up by Friday, June 6. A $2.00 late fee applies for each additional day after the deadline. Best, Reference Librarian, Greenway Library

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the cost of picking up the book late?

options.B: A $2.00 fee for each additional day
options.C: A blocked account until payment clears
options.D: A reinstated request through interlibrary loan

questions.explanation: The email specifies a $2.00 late fee for each additional day after the June 6 deadline.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email?

options.A: The book has been waiting at the desk for several days
options.B: The library reminds borrowers about pickup deadlines
options.C: The book will be returned automatically if uncollected
options.D: The library lacks staff to send late fee notices

questions.explanation: The email itself is a pickup reminder with deadline information, implying the library routinely reminds borrowers about deadlines.
